questions.questionText: The most common cause of microcytic hypochromic anemia worldwide is:
questions.explanation: Iron deficiency remains the commonest cause of microcytic anemia globally.

questions.questionText: The most specific laboratory marker of iron deficiency is:
questions.explanation: Low ferritin is the most specific indicator of depleted iron stores in the absence of inflammation.

questions.questionText: A megaloblastic anemia with neurologic deficits is most suggestive of deficiency of:
questions.explanation: Vitamin B12 deficiency causes megaloblastosis with neurologic manifestations due to demyelination.

questions.questionText: The peripheral smear finding classically seen in hereditary spherocytosis is:
questions.explanation: Hereditary spherocytosis shows spherocytes with increased osmotic fragility.

questions.questionText: A markedly elevated PT and aPTT with low fibrinogen and high D-dimer is most consistent with:
questions.explanation: Disseminated intravascular coagulation causes consumptive coagulopathy and secondary fibrinolysis.

questions.questionText: The most common inherited thrombophilia due to APC resistance is:
questions.explanation: Factor V Leiden is the most prevalent inherited thrombophilia in many populations.

questions.questionText: Auer rods on peripheral smear are classically associated with:
questions.explanation: Auer rods are myeloid inclusions and strongly support a diagnosis of AML.

questions.questionText: The cytogenetic abnormality t(15;17) is seen in:
questions.explanation: Acute promyelocytic leukemia has t(15;17) and responds to ATRA-based therapy.

questions.questionText: The hallmark molecular abnormality in CML is:
questions.explanation: CML is defined by Philadelphia chromosome t(9;22) producing BCR-ABL tyrosine kinase.

questions.questionText: Imatinib is a targeted therapy primarily used in:
questions.explanation: Imatinib inhibits BCR-ABL tyrosine kinase and transformed CML outcomes.

questions.questionText: An isolated thrombocytopenia with normal coagulation profile and mucocutaneous bleeding suggests:
questions.optionD: TTP with hemolysis in all cases
questions.explanation: ITP often presents as isolated low platelets with normal PT/aPTT and mucocutaneous bleeding.

questions.questionText: The classic pentad of TTP includes thrombocytopenia and microangiopathic hemolytic anemia plus:
questions.explanation: TTP classically has MAHA, thrombocytopenia, neurologic changes, renal injury, and fever.
